# enot

> An earthy color scheme for vim, WezTerm, Midnight Commander and
> ranger: dark and light themes, gruvbox character, built in CIELAB
> and machine-checked to stay distinguishable under color blindness -
> minimum pairwise CIEDE2000 of 8.2 between syntax accents and 7.2
> across the 16 ANSI colors, verified against simulated protanopia
> and deuteranopia.

The name is the Russian word for raccoon; every color is generated by
an optimizer, none are hand-picked. Dichromacy is simulated with
Vienot 1999 and Brettel 1997 matrices in linear sRGB; a regression
gate fails the build if any guarantee drops.

## Scheme files

- [enot.vim](https://enot-theme.github.io/enot.vim): vim/neovim colorscheme, dark and light in one file
- [enot-dark.toml](https://enot-theme.github.io/enot-dark.toml): WezTerm scheme, dark
- [enot-light.toml](https://enot-theme.github.io/enot-light.toml): WezTerm scheme, light
- [enot-dark-16M.ini](https://enot-theme.github.io/enot-dark-16M.ini) and
  [enot-light-16M.ini](https://enot-theme.github.io/enot-light-16M.ini): mc skins, true color
- [enot-dark256.ini](https://enot-theme.github.io/enot-dark256.ini) and
  [enot-light256.ini](https://enot-theme.github.io/enot-light256.ini): mc skins, xterm-256 fallback
- [enot.py](https://enot-theme.github.io/enot.py): ranger colorscheme, both themes via the
  terminal ANSI palette
- [colors.json](https://enot-theme.github.io/colors.json): the color spec - roles at three depths
  (true color, xterm-256, ANSI 16) with measured invariant metrics

## Documentation

- [Install for vim / neovim](https://enot-theme.github.io/vim/)
- [Install for WezTerm](https://enot-theme.github.io/wezterm/)
- [Install for Midnight Commander](https://enot-theme.github.io/mc/)
- [Install for ranger](https://enot-theme.github.io/ranger/)
- [Applications and coverage](https://enot-theme.github.io/apps/)
- [All install targets](https://enot-theme.github.io/get/)
- [Why warm themes collapse under color blindness](https://enot-theme.github.io/why/)
- [Guarantees and measured invariants](https://enot-theme.github.io/numbers/)
- [Method: how the palette is optimized](https://enot-theme.github.io/method/)
- [Published work it stands on](https://enot-theme.github.io/science/)
- [Homepage with live dichromacy simulation](https://enot-theme.github.io/)

## Optional

- [llms-full.txt](https://enot-theme.github.io/llms-full.txt): full documentation and all palette values
